---
title: "Chapter 2 - Sankhya Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">साङ्ख्ययोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-01.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-03.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-2-1" data-reference="2.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-1">2.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Seeing him thus overcome by compassion, his eyes troubled and brimming with tears, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusudana</span> spoke these words to the despondent <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् ।<br>
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-2" data-reference="2.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-2">2.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whence has this faintness come upon you in the hour of crisis, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>? It is ignoble, unworthy of an honorable man, and leads neither to heaven nor to fame.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।<br>
अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-3" data-reference="2.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-3">2.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Do not give way to weakness, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>; it does not become you.<br>
Cast off this base faint-heartedness and rise up, scorcher of foes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते ।<br>
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-4" data-reference="2.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-4">2.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">How, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusudana</span>, shall I fight back with arrows against Bhishma and Drona, who are worthy of reverence, O destroyer of enemies?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन ।<br>
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-5" data-reference="2.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-5">2.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Better to live in this world on alms than to kill these noble teachers. Were I to kill these teachers, though they seek their own gain, the very pleasures I enjoyed would be smeared with blood.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्<br>
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके ।<br>
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव<br>
भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-6" data-reference="2.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-6">2.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And we do not know which is better for us—that we should conquer them, or that they should conquer us; for the sons of Dhritarashtra stand before us, and having killed them we would have no wish to live.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो<br>
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः ।<br>
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम-<br>
स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-7" data-reference="2.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-7">2.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">My very being is stricken with the weakness of pity, my mind bewildered about my <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>. I ask you: tell me plainly what is best. I am your pupil; I take refuge in you—instruct me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः<br>
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः ।<br>
यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे<br>
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-8" data-reference="2.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-8">2.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For I see nothing that could drive away the grief withering my senses, even were I to win unrivaled and prosperous dominion on earth, or lordship over the gods.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्<br>
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् ।<br>
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं<br>
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-9" data-reference="2.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-9">2.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having spoken thus to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." aria-label="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." title="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." data-glossary-id="gudakesha">Gudakesha</span>, scorcher of foes, said to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." title="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="govinda">Govinda</span>, &quot;I will not fight,&quot; and fell silent.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप ।<br>
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-10" data-reference="2.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-10">2.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">To him, despondent there between the two armies, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span> spoke, as if smiling, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, these words:</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।<br>
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-11" data-reference="2.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-11">2.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You grieve for those who should not be grieved for, yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise grieve neither for the dead nor for the living.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे ।<br>
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-12" data-reference="2.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-12">2.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Never was there a time when I was not, nor you, nor these lords of men; nor shall any of us cease to be hereafter.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः ।<br>
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-13" data-reference="2.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-13">2.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As the embodied self passes, within this body, through childhood, youth, and age, so it passes to another body; the steady are not bewildered by this.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा ।<br>
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-14" data-reference="2.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-14">2.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The contacts of the senses with their objects, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, bring cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they come and go and do not last. Endure them, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः ।<br>
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-15" data-reference="2.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-15">2.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The one whom these do not trouble, O best of men, who is steady, alike in pain and pleasure—he is fit for immortality.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ ।<br>
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-16" data-reference="2.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-16">2.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of the unreal there is no being; of the real there is no ceasing to be.<br>
The seers of truth have discerned the conclusion of both.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः ।<br>
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-17" data-reference="2.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-17">2.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded; none can bring about the destruction of this imperishable one.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।<br>
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-18" data-reference="2.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-18">2.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">These bodies of the eternal embodied self are said to have an end, but the self itself is indestructible and beyond measure; therefore fight, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः ।<br>
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-19" data-reference="2.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-19">2.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who thinks it the slayer, and he who thinks it slain—neither understands; it neither slays nor is slain.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् ।<br>
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-20" data-reference="2.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-20">2.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It is never born and never dies;<br>
having once been, it never ceases to be.<br>
Unborn, eternal, everlasting, primeval,<br>
it is not killed when the body is killed.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्<br>
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः ।<br>
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो<br>
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-21" data-reference="2.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-21">2.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who knows it as indestructible, eternal, unborn, and unchanging—how can such a person kill, or cause to be killed, and whom, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् ।<br>
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-22" data-reference="2.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-22">2.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As a person casts off worn-out clothes and puts on others that are new, so the embodied self casts off worn-out bodies and takes on others that are new.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय<br>
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि ।<br>
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा-<br>
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-23" data-reference="2.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-23">2.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Weapons do not cut it, fire does not burn it; water does not wet it, nor does wind dry it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः ।<br>
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-24" data-reference="2.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-24">2.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried;<br>
it is eternal, all-pervading, firm, unmoving, and primeval.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च ।<br>
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-25" data-reference="2.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-25">2.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It is called unmanifest, unthinkable, and unchanging; therefore, knowing it to be such, you ought not to grieve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते ।<br>
तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-26" data-reference="2.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-26">2.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And even if you suppose it to be constantly born and constantly dying, even then, O mighty-armed one, you ought not to grieve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् ।<br>
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-27" data-reference="2.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-27">2.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one who is born, death is certain; for one who dies, birth is certain. Therefore you ought not to grieve over what cannot be avoided.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च ।<br>
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-28" data-reference="2.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-28">2.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Beings are unmanifest in their beginnings, manifest in the middle, and unmanifest again at their end, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>. What occasion for lament is there in this?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत ।<br>
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-29" data-reference="2.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-29">2.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One sees it as a wonder, another speaks of it as a wonder, another hears of it as a wonder; yet even having heard, no one truly knows it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन-<br>
माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः ।<br>
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श‍ृणोति<br>
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-30" data-reference="2.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-30">2.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The embodied self within the body of every being is forever beyond harm, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>; therefore you ought not to grieve for any being.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत ।<br>
तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-31" data-reference="2.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-31">2.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Considering, too, your own <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, you ought not to waver;<br>
for a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." aria-label="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." title="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." data-glossary-id="kshatriya">kshatriya</span> there is no greater good than a battle in accord with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि ।<br>
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-32" data-reference="2.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-32">2.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Happy the kshatriyas, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, who meet with such a battle, come of itself like an open gate to heaven.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् ।<br>
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-33" data-reference="2.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-33">2.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But if you will not wage this rightful battle, then, forsaking your own <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> and your honor, you will fall into sin.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि ।<br>
ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-34" data-reference="2.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-34">2.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">People will tell of your lasting disgrace; and for one who has been honored, disgrace is worse than death.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।<br>
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-35" data-reference="2.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-35">2.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The great chariot-warriors will think you withdrew from the battle out of fear, and you will fall in the esteem of those who held you high.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः ।<br>
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-36" data-reference="2.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-36">2.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Your enemies will speak many unseemly words, scorning your strength. What could be more painful than that?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः ।<br>
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-37" data-reference="2.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-37">2.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Slain, you will gain heaven; victorious, you will enjoy the earth. Therefore rise up, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, resolved to fight.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।<br>
तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-38" data-reference="2.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-38">2.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Holding pleasure and pain alike, gain and loss, victory and defeat, gird yourself for battle; thus you will incur no sin.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ ।<br>
ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-39" data-reference="2.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-39">2.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This understanding has been declared to you according to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span>; now hear it as it bears on <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>. Yoked with this understanding, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, you will cast off the bondage of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." aria-label="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." title="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." data-glossary-id="karma">karma</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श‍ृणु ।<br>
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-40" data-reference="2.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-40">2.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">On this path no effort is lost and no harm is done; even a little of this <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> protects one from great fear.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते ।<br>
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-41" data-reference="2.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-41">2.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Here there is but one resolute understanding, O joy of the Kurus; but the thoughts of the irresolute are many-branched and endless.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन ।<br>
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-42" data-reference="2.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-42">2.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This flowery speech is uttered by the unwise, who delight in the letter of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, and declare there is nothing more;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः ।<br>
वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-43" data-reference="2.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-43">2.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">full of desire and intent on heaven, they offer rites that yield rebirth as the fruit of action, abounding in special ceremonies aimed at enjoyment and power.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् ।<br>
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-44" data-reference="2.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-44">2.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those attached to enjoyment and power, whose minds are carried away by such talk, no resolute understanding is established in concentration.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् ।<br>
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-45" data-reference="2.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-45">2.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> treat of the three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>; be free of the three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>—beyond the pairs of opposites, ever poised in purity, indifferent to gain and safety, and possessed of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन ।<br>
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-46" data-reference="2.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-46">2.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As much use as there is in a well when water floods on every side, so much use is there in all the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> for the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." aria-label="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." title="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." data-glossary-id="brahmin">brahmin</span> who knows.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः सम्प्लुतोदके ।<br>
तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-47" data-reference="2.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-47">2.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Your right is to action alone, never to its fruits.<br>
Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let attachment to inaction take hold in you.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन ।<br>
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-48" data-reference="2.48">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-48">2.48</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, perform your actions, abandoning attachment, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>, even-minded in success and failure; this evenness of mind is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय ।<br>
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-49" data-reference="2.49">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-49">2.49</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Action is far inferior to the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of understanding, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>; take refuge in understanding. Pitiable are those who act for the sake of fruits.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय ।<br>
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-50" data-reference="2.50">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-50">2.50</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One yoked in understanding casts off here both good and evil deeds; therefore devote yourself to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>—<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> is skill in action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।<br>
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-51" data-reference="2.51">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-51">2.51</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The wise, yoked in understanding, renounce the fruit born of action and, freed from the bondage of birth, reach the state beyond all ills.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः ।<br>
जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-52" data-reference="2.52">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-52">2.52</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When your understanding has crossed beyond the thicket of delusion, then you will grow indifferent to what is yet to be heard and what has been heard.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति ।<br>
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-53" data-reference="2.53">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-53">2.53</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When your understanding, once bewildered by scriptural lore, stands unmoving and steady in concentration, then you will attain <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला ।<br>
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-54" data-reference="2.54">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-54">2.54</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">What is the mark of one whose wisdom is steady, settled in concentration, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span>? How does the steady-minded one speak, how sit, how move?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव ।<br>
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-55" data-reference="2.55">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-55">2.55</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When a person puts away all the desires that enter the mind, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, and is content within himself by himself, then he is called steady in wisdom.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् ।<br>
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-56" data-reference="2.56">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-56">2.56</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He whose mind is untroubled in sorrows and free of craving amid pleasures, gone beyond longing, fear, and anger, is called a sage of steady mind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः ।<br>
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-57" data-reference="2.57">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-57">2.57</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who is unattached on every side, who neither delights nor recoils at meeting good or ill—his wisdom stands firm.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् ।<br>
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-58" data-reference="2.58">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-58">2.58</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And when, like a tortoise drawing in its limbs, he withdraws his senses entirely from their objects, his wisdom stands firm.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-59" data-reference="2.59">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-59">2.59</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The objects of sense fall away from the embodied one who abstains, though a taste for them remains; even that taste falls away when he sees the Supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः ।<br>
रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-60" data-reference="2.60">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-60">2.60</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For even in one who strives, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, who sees clearly, the turbulent senses carry off the mind by force.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-61" data-reference="2.61">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-61">2.61</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Holding all these in check, let him sit yoked, intent on me; for one whose senses are mastered, wisdom stands firm.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः ।<br>
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-62" data-reference="2.62">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-62">2.62</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">In a person who dwells on the objects of sense, attachment to them is born;<br>
from attachment springs desire, and from desire, anger.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते ।<br>
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-63" data-reference="2.63">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-63">2.63</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">From anger comes delusion; from delusion, the wandering of memory;<br>
from the loss of memory, the ruin of understanding; and from the ruin of understanding, the person perishes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः ।<br>
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-64" data-reference="2.64">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-64">2.64</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But one who moves among the objects of sense with senses freed from attachment and aversion, mastered and under his own control, attains serenity.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रागद्वेषविमुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् ।<br>
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-65" data-reference="2.65">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-65">2.65</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">In serenity all his sorrows are dissolved; for the mind of the serene soon grows steady.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते ।<br>
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-66" data-reference="2.66">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-66">2.66</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the unsteady there is no understanding, and for the unsteady, no meditation;<br>
without meditation there is no peace, and for one without peace, how can there be happiness?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना ।<br>
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-67" data-reference="2.67">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-67">2.67</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For when the mind runs after the roving senses, it carries off one&#x27;s wisdom as the wind carries off a boat upon the water.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते ।<br>
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-68" data-reference="2.68">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-68">2.68</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore, O mighty-armed one, he whose senses are wholly withdrawn from their objects—his wisdom stands firm.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-69" data-reference="2.69">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-69">2.69</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">What is night for all beings is the waking of the disciplined one;<br>
where beings are awake, there it is night for the sage who sees.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी ।<br>
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-70" data-reference="2.70">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-70">2.70</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As the waters flow into the ocean, which fills yet stays unmoved, so all desires flow into him who attains peace—not the one who hankers after desires.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं<br>
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् ।<br>
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे<br>
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-71" data-reference="2.71">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-71">2.71</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The person who, abandoning all desires, moves about free of longing, without &#x27;mine&#x27; and without ego, attains peace.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः ।<br>
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-2-72" data-reference="2.72">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-2-72">2.72</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This is the state of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>; reaching it, one is no longer deluded.<br>
Established in it even at the hour of death, one attains the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvana</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति ।<br>
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
